mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, equity discount rates ease faster than earnings expectations, so S&P 500 prices should rise over the next month. This signal is invalid if rate cuts coincide with deteriorating inflation or activity data that overwhelm the valuation effect.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below the unconditional mean in more than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS is declining, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above prior trend implies the Fed will tighten policy sooner, lifting discount rates and pressuring equity multiples. The effect should be negative over the next month when inflation surprise is broad-based and not offset by stronger real activity.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the negative return spread versus non-triggered periods is not statistically distinct.

mechanism_narrative: A falling UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS indicates labor-market improvement that supports consumption and earnings revisions, creating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal should weaken if hiring gains are accompanied by faster inflation and expected tightening.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint condition of lower UNRATE and higher PAYEMS does not outperform single-series signals, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-signal observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS above its recent trend indicates stronger nominal consumer spending, which should lift revenue expectations and equity prices over the next week. The effect is strongest when the rebound is broad and not purely price-driven.
failure_condition: Rejected if weekly SPX returns after RSAFS threshold crossings are not positive on average, or if the hit rate is below 50% over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO signals improving industrial supply capacity and stronger cyclical output, which should support risk assets over a one-month horizon through better earnings expectations and lower recession odds. The mechanism fails when output gains are offset by a sharp rise in financing costs.
failure_condition: Rejected if consecutive INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the positive return spread disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST above its recent base signals housing-demand resilience and a wider pro-cyclical expansion, which should lift equities over a quarter through wealth effects and construction-linked earnings. The signal is invalid if housing strength occurs alongside aggressive policy tightening.
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline following housing breakouts, or if the signal loses significance when FEDFUNDS is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises while PAYEMS stalls or contracts, labor slack is increasing and recession risk is rising, so S&P 500 should fall over the next month. The effect should be strongest when the labor deterioration is broad across successive monthly releases.
failure_condition: Rejected if labor-deterioration triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power beyond either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, CPIAUCSL acceleration combined with a low but rising FEDFUNDS rate implies the Fed is behind inflation, so equity multiples compress and forward returns turn negative over the next 60 days. The signal fails when inflation falls faster than policy tightens.
failure_condition: Rejected if the CPI-FedFunds interaction does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns in tightening regimes, or if rolling 3-year IC remains below 0.03.
